description: baseline failed because autoresearch runner could not spawn bash for ./autoresearch.sh

asi.hypothesis: Initial benchmark harness using a bash wrapper would establish baseline metrics for agent latency phases.
asi.error: run_experiment reported spawn bash ENOENT before executing ./autoresearch.sh.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark did not start; likely Windows environment lacks bash for run_experiment command wrapper or script shebang.
asi.next_action_hint: Switch autoresearch command to a direct Bun invocation or provide a Windows-compatible runner so run_experiment does not depend on bash.

description: baseline failed loading pi-natives native module under Windows Bun

asi.hypothesis: After making bash available to run_experiment, the benchmark would execute and produce baseline latency metrics.
asi.error: Bun could not resolve pi_natives.win32-x64.node from packages/natives/native/index.js; suggested rebuild or reinstall @oh-my-pi/pi-natives.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark process exited before metrics due missing native binding.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid pi-natives-dependent imports in the benchmark or build packages/natives; easiest is to remove SearchTool/native grep and renderer imports that transitively load sanitizeText/pi-natives from the benchmark.

description: baseline for native-independent MCP tool-discovery latency workload

asi.hypothesis: Establish a stable baseline for pure MCP tool-discovery search, result formatting, and server summary latency before optimizing ranking algorithms.
asi.environment_note: run_experiment required a bash.exe shim in the user agent bin path on Windows; benchmark emits warnings about missing /tmp but completes successfully.
asi.bottleneck_hint: BM25 query time and repeated result formatting dominate the workload; both call searchDiscoverableMCPTools many times, so avoiding full sort/allocation for top-k should be promising.

description: use bounded top-k BM25 ranking and filter selected MCP tools during search

asi.hypothesis: For small result limits, keeping only the best k BM25 matches while scanning avoids allocating, sorting, and slicing every positive document; SearchToolBm25 can pass selected tools as an exclusion set instead of ranking the entire corpus first.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved ~28.7%; bm25_query_ms dropped strongly while result_format_ms rose slightly because that phase performs more repeated searches and noise shifted work distribution.
asi.semantic_contract: Ranking order remains score descending with tool-name ascending tie-break; checksum unchanged, suggesting result set stability for benchmark queries.
asi.next_action_hint: Profile index build and tokenization next; repeated Array.from/reduce and tokenize regex passes may be reducible.

description: track BM25 document length while tokenizing instead of reducing map values

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding Array.from(termFrequencies.values()).reduce during index construction should reduce per-document allocation and index-build latency without changing weighted length semantics.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved further to 3291ms; bm25_index_ms was roughly flat/noisy, but query and formatting phases also improved with unchanged checksum.
asi.semantic_contract: Document length still equals the sum of weighted token occurrences because addWeightedTokens increments length by weight for every token occurrence.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider manual tokenization or precomputed query statistics; regex tokenize still allocates multiple intermediate strings per field/query.

description: replace regex tokenization with manual ASCII scanner

asi.hypothesis: Manual ASCII tokenization would avoid regex replace/split allocations and speed query/index phases while preserving camelCase and non-alphanumeric splitting semantics.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed from 3291ms to 3669ms; bm25_query_ms, bm25_index_ms, and result_format_ms all worsened despite unchanged checksum.
asi.rollback_reason: Manual scanner used per-character string concatenation and a closure, which is slower than Bun's optimized regex/string pipeline for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry this manual tokenizer shape; if revisiting tokenization, try caching tokenized queries or field tokens rather than hand-scanning strings.

description: precompute BM25 document normalization in the search index

asi.hypothesis: Precomputing per-document BM25 length normalization during index build would avoid repeated arithmetic in query scoring.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed badly to 5696ms and all major phases worsened, likely due optional property/shape changes on search documents and extra index-build writes.
asi.rollback_reason: Optimization made hot document objects slower despite removing arithmetic; checksum unchanged but latency worse.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid adding optional hot-path fields to DiscoverableMCPSearchDocument. If precomputing, consider a parallel numeric array benchmarked carefully, but focus first on algorithmic scoring/skipping.

description: avoid redundant Set allocation when computing document frequencies

asi.hypothesis: Because termFrequencies is already a Map with unique keys, iterating keys directly should avoid per-document Set allocation during index build.
asi.observed_effect: bm25_index_ms improved slightly, but primary regressed heavily due much slower query/result-format phases in the noisy full workload.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened; small index-build gain did not justify overall regression/noise risk.
asi.next_action_hint: This change is logically sound but benchmark showed instability; retry only with isolated index-build benchmark or after reducing noise, not in the current primary workload.

description: cache the current worst top-k BM25 result until replacement

asi.hypothesis: The bounded top-k loop was still rescanning the k-sized result buffer for every positive candidate; caching the current worst result and recomputing it only after replacement should remove most O(n*k) overhead.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 2345ms; bm25_query_ms and result_format_ms dropped sharply with unchanged checksum. summary_ms worsened but is secondary/noisy.
asi.semantic_contract: Top-k ordering remains unchanged because the cached worst index is invalidated after every insertion or replacement and final results are sorted by the same comparator.
asi.next_action_hint: Further top-k gains may come from avoiding score calculation for documents with no query-token overlap or precomputing query IDF values once per search.

description: precompute BM25 query IDF values once per search

asi.hypothesis: Computing documentFrequency lookups and IDF logarithms once per query token should remove repeated Math.log/map work from the per-document scoring loop.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 1372ms; bm25_query_ms nearly halved and result_format_ms dropped sharply. bm25_index_ms worsened/noisy but secondary only.
asi.semantic_contract: Tokens absent from documentFrequencies can be skipped because no document can contribute a positive term frequency for them; checksum stayed unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Next scoring optimization: skip documents that have none of the weighted query terms before computing normalization/result objects, or reduce normalization arithmetic.

description: compute BM25 length normalization once per matching document

asi.hypothesis: Documents matching multiple query terms currently recompute length normalization per matched term; caching it in a local variable should reduce arithmetic.
asi.observed_effect: Primary regressed to 2907ms; query and formatting phases worsened, and unrelated index/summary metrics were much noisier. Checksum unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra branch/local state in the hot scoring loop outweighed the saved arithmetic for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add lazy normalization branches in the scorer. Focus on reducing document scans or data structure lookups instead.

description: add postings lists to MCP BM25 index to score only matching documents

asi.hypothesis: An inverted postings map lets BM25 search visit only documents containing query terms instead of scanning every document and doing per-query-token map lookups.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 751ms; bm25_query_ms and result_format_ms dropped dramatically while index build remained comparable. Checksum unchanged.
asi.tradeoff: Index stores postings arrays in addition to per-document term maps, increasing memory somewhat for faster repeated search. This is acceptable for discoverable MCP tool sets and cached search indexes.
asi.semantic_contract: Fallback scan path remains for externally constructed indexes without postings; rankings still use the same score formula and comparator.
asi.next_action_hint: Optimize summary generation or postings score accumulation next; current hot path may be Map<document, score> accumulation.

description: store posting document indices and accumulate BM25 scores in arrays

asi.hypothesis: Using numeric document indices in postings plus sparse score/touched arrays should reduce Map<object, number> overhead during postings-based scoring.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved modestly to 714ms; query and formatting phases improved, while index and summary metrics worsened slightly/noisily. Checksum unchanged.
asi.tradeoff: Postings now dereference documents by index, reducing per-posting object references but still keeping the optional postings map fallback boundary.
asi.next_action_hint: Try reducing repeated exclusion Set lookups or closure overhead in pushResult; gains are now smaller, so confirm with repeat if near noise floor.

description: hoist excluded MCP tool set lookup out of BM25 scoring loops

asi.hypothesis: Hoisting options?.excludedToolNames out of the inner postings and fallback loops should reduce optional-chain/property lookup overhead, especially when no exclusions are supplied.
asi.observed_effect: Primary improved to 615ms with better query, formatting, index, and summary medians; checksum unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: With query scoring now much faster, index-build and summary phases are meaningful; consider optimizing summary caching or index construction, but beware previous index-only changes were noisy.
